content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges [(0, 1, 0), (1, 2, 1), (0, 2, 2), (0, 7, 0), (1, 2, 0), (0, 4, 8), (4, 9, 8), (0, 6, 9), (2, 4, 12), (1, 4, 14), (1, 9, 16), (3, 9, 20), (0, 5, 21), (0, 3, 22), (3, 5, 27)], we need to find when node 2 and node 1 are first connected.

We will scan each edge and collect the time t whenever u and v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

First, we will scan the edge (0, 1, 0) and collect the time 0.
Next, we will scan the edge (1, 2, 1) and collect the time 1.
Then, we will scan the edge (0, 2, 2) and collect the time 2.
After that, we will scan the edge (0, 7, 0) and collect the time 0.
Next, we will scan the edge (1, 2, 0) and collect the time 1.
Then, we will scan the edge (0, 4, 8) and collect the time 8.
After that, we will scan the edge (4, 9, 8) and collect the time 8.
Next, we will scan the edge (0, 6, 9) and collect the time 9.
Then, we will scan the edge (2, 4, 12) and collect the time 12.
After that, we will scan the edge (1, 4, 14) and collect the time 14.
Next, we will scan the edge (1, 9, 16) and collect the time 16.
Then, we will scan the edge (3, 9, 20) and collect the time 20.
After that, we will scan the edge (0, 5, 21) and collect the time 21.
Next, we will scan the edge (0, 3, 22) and collect the time 22.
Finally, we will scan the edge (3, 5, 27) and collect the time 27.

Now we have collected all the times t whenever u and v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

Answer: 16
